title: Secrets, Dependencies, Static Analysis, and Supply Chain · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Build layered security gates for source, dependencies, workflows, and releases. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a secure-development gate stack after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent, detect, revoke, and remediate committed credentials and sensitive files.
Scan direct and transitive dependencies and triage exploitability and reachability.
Use static security analysis for data flow and dangerous patterns with reviewed queries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Secret Scanning · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Secret Scanning without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: Prevent, detect, revoke, and remediate committed credentials and sensitive files. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Secret Scanning, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a secure-development gate stack.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting a secret from the latest commit removes its exposure. This fails the Secret Scanning contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent, detect, revoke, and remediate committed credentials and sensitive files.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Artifact Provenance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A release tag proves artifact integrity.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record build provenance, checksums, signatures, and dependency inventories when risk requires. Artifact under review: a secure-development gate stack.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Artifact Provenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Artifact Provenance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record build provenance, checksums, signatures, and dependency inventories when risk requires.
